education.title: How to Help Iran: What You Need to Know
education.description: Understanding the humanitarian crisis in Iran is the first step toward making a difference. Learn about the war, protests, sanctions impact, and how your support reaches those in need.
education.y2020: Sanctions intensify, compounding economic hardship. Humanitarian organizations warn of a growing Iran crisis affecting civilian access to food and medicine.
education.y2021: Floods and earthquakes compound existing challenges. International Iran humanitarian aid efforts expand to meet surging needs.
education.y2022: Mahsa Amini protests erupt nationwide. The Woman Life Freedom movement draws global attention to Iran human rights abuses and political prisoners.
education.y2023: IIRan founded as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. First humanitarian aid shipments reach affected communities in multiple provinces.
education.y2024: Regional conflict escalates. Rebuilding programs launched in 12 provinces; education centers reopened for children displaced by the Iran crisis.
education.y2025: Israel strikes Iranian nuclear sites in June; US strikes follow. The Twelve-Day War causes mass civilian casualties. Over 2 million people supported through Iran relief fund operations.
education.y2026: Mass protests since December 2025. Severe government crackdown with 16,500+ killed. Internet blackout isolates millions. Iran rebuilding and humanitarian aid more urgent than ever.
education.faqHowHelp: How can I help Iran right now?
education.faqHowHelpA: The most immediate way to help Iran is to donate to verified humanitarian organizations like IIRan that deliver food, medical supplies, and shelter to Iranian civilians. You can also raise awareness by sharing verified news, contacting elected officials to advocate for civilian protection, and supporting Iranian diaspora community events and fundraisers.
education.faqCrisis: What is the current humanitarian crisis in Iran?
education.faqCrisisA: Iran faces a multi-layered humanitarian crisis in 2026. Military strikes in mid-2025 damaged critical infrastructure. Protests since December 2025 have been met with lethal force, with over 16,500 killed and 330,000 injured. International sanctions compound economic hardship, limiting access to food, clean water, and medicine for millions. An internet blackout has isolated entire communities from the outside world.
education.faqDonation: Where does my donation to Iran go?
education.faqDonationA: 91% of every dollar donated goes directly to programs: food packages, emergency medical supplies, shelter materials, clean water systems, and educational resources for children. All spending is tracked and reported in quarterly impact reports. IIRan partners with established international NGOs (UN OCHA, ICRC, UNICEF) and vetted local organizations with verified distribution records.
education.faqLegit: Is IIRan a legitimate charity for Iran relief?
education.faqLegitA: Yes. IIRan, Inc. is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit. We are non-partisan and do not support, endorse, or oppose any political entity. Our sole focus is the well-being of Iranian civilians affected by crisis. All financial records are publicly available.
education.faqWLF: What is the Woman, Life, Freedom movement in Iran?
education.faqWLFA: Woman, Life, Freedom (Zan, Zendegi, Azadi) is a protest movement that began after the death of Mahsa Amini in September 2022 while in custody of Iran's morality police. It has become a broader call for Iran human rights, women's rights, and an end to compulsory veiling. The movement has drawn global solidarity and remains central to ongoing protests in 2025-2026.
education.faqSanctions: How do sanctions impact ordinary people in Iran?
education.faqSanctionsA: International sanctions on Iran, while targeting the government, have severe ripple effects on civilians. They restrict access to imported medicines, medical equipment, and food supplies. Iran's currency has lost over 80% of its value, pushing millions below the poverty line. Humanitarian exemptions exist but are difficult to navigate, creating gaps that organizations like IIRan work to fill.
education.faqVolunteer: Can I volunteer or help Iran in ways other than donating?
education.faqVolunteerA: Yes. Beyond financial donations, you can volunteer for awareness campaigns, share verified news from Iran on social media, organize community fundraisers, write to elected officials urging civilian protection, or provide professional services (medical, legal, educational) through our partner network. Every action to amplify Iranian voices makes a difference.

seo.title: How to Help Iran: Support Iranian People Today
seo.intro: Millions of Iranians face the worst humanitarian crisis in decades. War, sanctions, and a brutal crackdown on protests have left civilians without food, medicine, and shelter. Here is how you can make a difference right now.
seo.donateText: Your donation funds food, medical care, clean water, and shelter for Iranian civilians. 91% of every dollar goes directly to programs.
seo.awarenessText: Share verified Iran news on social media, attend solidarity events, and amplify the voices of Iranians facing a government crackdown and internet blackout.
seo.volunteerText: Medical professionals, translators, legal experts, and educators can contribute through our partner network to support Iranian people directly.
seo.advocateText: Contact elected officials, support resolutions protecting Iranian civilians, and stand with the Woman, Life, Freedom movement for Iran human rights.
seo.crisisP1: In June 2025, military strikes targeted nuclear facilities and critical infrastructure across Iran, triggering the Twelve-Day War. Civilian casualties surged as hospitals, power grids, and water systems were damaged. The economic toll of intensified sanctions has pushed millions below the poverty line.
seo.crisisP2: Since December 2025, mass protests have swept Iranian cities calling for political change. Security forces have responded with lethal force, with reports of over 16,500 killed and 330,000 injured. A nationwide internet blackout has cut communities off from the world, making independent reporting nearly impossible.
seo.crisisP3: Children, women, and minorities bear the heaviest burden of the crisis. The Woman, Life, Freedom movement continues to demand basic human rights despite extreme repression. Iran political prisoners number in the tens of thousands.
seo.howWeDeliverP1: IIRan is a registered 501(c)(3) Iran charity that coordinates with UN OCHA, ICRC, UNHCR, WHO, and UNICEF to deliver humanitarian aid where it is needed most. We operate an Iran relief fund that has reached 2.4 million people across 48 countries.
seo.howWeDeliverP2: Our programs focus on five pillars: emergency food distribution, medical care and supplies, clean water access, emergency shelter, and education for displaced children. Every donation is tracked with full transparency in our quarterly impact reports.
seo.howWeDeliverP3: Whether you want to donate to Iran relief, volunteer your professional skills, or advocate for Iran peace and human rights, IIRan provides verified, accountable channels to support the Iranian people. Together, we can help Iran rebuild.

about.heading: About IIRan
about.intro1: IIRan is an independent, non-partisan humanitarian organization dedicated to supporting peace, recovery, and rebuilding in Iran. Founded in 2023 by a coalition of Iranian humanitarian professionals and diaspora leaders, we have delivered over $18 million in aid to 2.4 million people across 31 provinces.
about.intro2: We are a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization with global operations.
about.radicalTransparencyDesc: Every dollar is tracked. Quarterly impact reports are published publicly. Our finances are independently audited annually.
about.nonPartisanDesc: We do not support, endorse, or oppose any political entity. Aid is distributed based solely on need, without discrimination.
about.localEmpowermentDesc: We work through established local organizations, building capacity rather than dependency. Communities lead their own recovery.
about.accountabilityDesc: Governed by an independent board. Subject to external audits. Compliant with international humanitarian standards.
about.boardDescription: Our independent board provides governance, financial oversight, and strategic direction. All board members serve pro bono.
about.leadershipDescription: Our leadership team brings decades of combined experience in humanitarian operations, finance, public health, and communications.
about.partnersNote: Partner relationships verified through formal memoranda of understanding. View our partnership documentation.
about.questionsText: We welcome inquiries from donors, journalists, potential partners, beneficiaries, and anyone who wants to learn more about our work.

contact.heading: Get in Touch
contact.description: We are here to answer your questions. Whether you are a donor, journalist, potential partner, or someone who wants to help, we would love to hear from you.
contact.generalDesc: For general questions about our mission and programs.
contact.donationsDesc: Questions about your donation, tax receipts, or planned giving.
contact.pressDesc: Media inquiries, interview requests, and press kit access.
contact.partnershipsDesc: For organizations interested in collaboration or joint programs.
contact.volunteerDesc: Volunteering opportunities, internships, and open positions.
contact.emergencyResponse: Email info@iiran.org with "URGENT" in the subject line

donatePage.heading: Donate to Help Iranian Civilians
donatePage.subheading: Send USDT to directly fund food, medical care, and shelter for families in crisis.
donatePage.scanOrCopy: Scan the QR code or copy the address
donatePage.onlySendWarning: Only send USDT on TRC-20. Other tokens or networks may cause loss of funds.
donatePage.noUsdtYet: Don't have USDT yet? We'll show you how (takes 10 min)
donatePage.whyCryptoTransparent: Fully verifiable on the public blockchain
donatePage.howToBuy: How to Buy USDT with US Dollars
donatePage.howToBuyDesc: USDT is a stablecoin worth exactly $1. Buying it is as simple as using a money transfer app. Here is how:
donatePage.step1Title: Step 1: Sign up on an exchange
donatePage.step1Desc: Pick any exchange below. Sign up takes 5 minutes.
donatePage.step2Title: Step 2: Buy USDT with your card or bank
donatePage.step2Desc: USDT is a stablecoin worth exactly $1. Pay with card, bank transfer, or Apple/Google Pay.
donatePage.step3Title: Step 3: Withdraw USDT to our wallet
donatePage.step3Desc: Send your USDT to the address at the top of this page. Select the TRC-20 (TRON) network.
donatePage.withdrawNote: Not sure how to withdraw? Here are guides for each exchange:
donatePage.networkWarning: Make sure you select TRC-20 (TRON) as the network when withdrawing. Arrives in 1-3 minutes, fees under $1.
donatePage.faqSubtitle: Common questions about donating with cryptocurrency.
donatePage.faqWhatIsUsdt: What is USDT and why do you use it?
donatePage.faqWhatIsUsdtA: USDT (Tether) is a stablecoin - a cryptocurrency whose value is pegged 1:1 to the US dollar. If you send $50 in USDT, we receive exactly $50. Unlike Bitcoin or Ethereum, there is no price volatility, so your intended donation amount is what we receive. We use the TRC-20 (TRON) network because it offers the fastest settlement and lowest fees - often under $1.
donatePage.faqNeverUsedCrypto: I have never used crypto before. Is it difficult?
donatePage.faqNeverUsedCryptoA: Not at all. Buying USDT is similar to using a money transfer app. Create an account on an exchange (Binance, Coinbase, etc.), deposit money from your bank or card, buy USDT, and send it to our address. Most exchanges have step-by-step guides. The entire process takes about 10-15 minutes the first time.
donatePage.faqVerify: Can I verify my donation on the blockchain?
donatePage.faqVerifyA: Yes. Every transaction on the TRON network is permanently recorded on a public ledger. After sending, you can search the wallet address on Tronscan.org to see your transaction and all fund movements. This is one of the key advantages of crypto donations - full transparency.
donatePage.faqOtherCryptoA: Currently we accept only USDT on the TRC-20 network. This ensures we receive a stable dollar value with minimal fees. We may expand to additional tokens in the future.
donatePage.faqProperUse: How do I know my donation will be used properly?
donatePage.faqProperUseA: IIRan is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit. All donations are tracked on-chain and allocated to verified humanitarian programs. We publish transparency reports detailing fund allocation. You can follow the wallet address on any block explorer to see all transactions.
donatePage.faqWrongToken: What if I send the wrong token or use the wrong network?
donatePage.faqWrongTokenA: Please only send USDT on the TRC-20 network. Sending other tokens or using a different network (such as ERC-20 or BEP-20) may result in permanent loss of funds. Always double-check the network selection before confirming.
donatePage.everyContributionDesc: Whether $10 or $500, your donation directly funds food, medical supplies, shelter, and education for Iranian families in need.

accessibility.heading: Accessibility Statement
accessibility.commitmentText: IIRan is committed to ensuring that our website is accessible to the widest possible audience, including people with disabilities. We actively work to increase the accessibility and usability of our website and hold ourselves to the standards of the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 Level AA.
accessibility.semanticHtmlDesc: We use proper heading hierarchy, landmarks, and ARIA attributes to ensure screen readers can navigate our content effectively.
accessibility.keyboardNavDesc: All interactive elements are accessible via keyboard. Focus indicators are visible on all focusable elements.
accessibility.colorContrastDesc: Text and interactive elements meet WCAG AA contrast ratios (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text).
accessibility.altTextDesc: All images include descriptive alt text. Decorative images are properly marked as presentation-only.
accessibility.responsiveDesignDesc: Our website is fully functional at zoom levels up to 400% and on all screen sizes.
accessibility.reducedMotionDesc: We respect the "prefers-reduced-motion" operating system setting and minimize animations when enabled.
accessibility.darkModeDesc: Full dark mode support that respects system preferences.
accessibility.formLabelsDesc: All form inputs have associated labels and error messages are clearly announced.
accessibility.conformanceText: We aim to conform to WCAG 2.2 Level AA. While we strive for full conformance, some third-party content (embedded videos, external news sources) may not fully meet all criteria. We are working with content providers to improve accessibility.
accessibility.limitation1: Some embedded YouTube videos may lack captions or audio descriptions.
accessibility.limitation2: PDF documents linked from external sources may not be fully accessible.
accessibility.limitation3: The interactive global support map has limited screen reader support; we provide a text-based alternative.
accessibility.feedbackText: If you encounter any accessibility barriers on our website, please let us know. We take all feedback seriously and will work to resolve issues promptly.
accessibility.assessmentText: IIRan assesses the accessibility of our website through a combination of automated testing tools, manual testing with assistive technologies, and periodic third-party audits. Our most recent audit was conducted in March 2026 by an independent accessibility specialist.

ethics.heading: Ethics & Whistleblower Policy
ethics.reportText: If you are a staff member, volunteer, partner, beneficiary, or member of the public and you have witnessed or suspect misconduct, fraud, abuse, or any violation of our policies, we encourage you to report it. All reports are treated confidentially.
ethics.encrypted: encrypted, monitored by Board Audit Committee
ethics.anonymous: Anonymous reports accepted. No account or identification required.
ethics.codeOfEthicsText: All IIRan board members, staff, volunteers, and partners are bound by our Code of Ethics, which requires:
ethics.integrityDesc: Honest and transparent conduct in all operations
ethics.nonDiscriminationDesc: Aid distributed based solely on need, without discrimination by race, religion, nationality, gender, or political affiliation
ethics.accountabilityDesc: Responsible stewardship of donor funds and organizational resources
ethics.respectDesc: Dignity and respect for all beneficiaries, colleagues, and partners
ethics.complianceDesc: Adherence to all applicable laws, regulations, and IIRan policies
ethics.conflictDesc: Prompt disclosure of any real or perceived conflicts of interest
ethics.whatToReportText: We encourage reporting of any suspected:
ethics.report1: Financial fraud, embezzlement, or misuse of donor funds
ethics.report3: Diversion of humanitarian aid for non-intended purposes
ethics.report5: Discrimination or harassment of any kind
ethics.report6: Violations of donor privacy or data protection
ethics.report7: Safety and security threats to staff or beneficiaries
ethics.report8: Falsification of reports, data, or financial records
ethics.report9: Any violation of IIRan policies or applicable law
ethics.whistleblowerText: IIRan strictly prohibits retaliation against anyone who, in good faith, reports a suspected violation. Protections include:
ethics.confidentialityDesc: Reporter identity is protected to the maximum extent possible
ethics.anonymityDesc: Reports can be made anonymously. We will investigate regardless.
ethics.noRetaliationDesc: Any employee who retaliates against a whistleblower is subject to immediate disciplinary action, up to and including termination
ethics.legalProtectionDesc: Whistleblowers are protected under applicable laws and international humanitarian accountability standards
ethics.receiptDesc: All reports are received by the Board Audit Committee, independent of management.
ethics.assessmentDesc: Within 48 hours, the Committee assesses the report and determines the appropriate course of action.
ethics.investigationDesc: An investigation is conducted by the Committee or an independent third party. Investigations are completed within 30 days when possible.
ethics.resolutionDesc: Findings and corrective actions are documented. Reporters receive an outcome summary (unless anonymous).
ethics.reportingDesc: The Board receives quarterly summaries of all ethics reports and resolutions.
ethics.governanceText: The Board Audit Committee has independent authority to investigate ethics complaints, engage external investigators or legal counsel, and recommend corrective action. The Committee reports directly to the full Board of Directors, not to management.
ethics.externalReportingText: If you believe your concern has not been adequately addressed internally, you may also report to:
ethics.chsDesc: Report concerns to the Core Humanitarian Standard Alliance
ethics.hqaiDesc: Independent quality assurance body for humanitarian organizations
ethics.ochaDesc: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs

press.heading: Media Resources
press.description: We work with journalists and media organizations to share accurate, timely information about the humanitarian situation in Iran. All press inquiries receive a response within 4 hours during business hours.
press.mediaCoverageDesc: Selected coverage from regional and international media outlets.
press.mediaKitDesc: Download our media kit for logos, brand guidelines, fact sheets, and high-resolution photos approved for editorial use.
press.brandGuidelinesDesc: SVG, PNG, PDF formats. Light and dark variants.
press.factSheetDesc: One-page overview with key statistics and mission statement.
press.photoLibraryDesc: High-resolution photos cleared for editorial use.
press.leadershipBiosDesc: Official biographies and photos of key personnel.
press.requestInterviewDesc: Our Executive Director and program directors are available for interviews. We can provide on-the-ground context, data, and expert analysis on the Iran humanitarian situation.

privacy.intro: IIRan ("we," "us," or "our") is committed to protecting the privacy and security of your personal information. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our website iiran.org, make a donation, or interact with our services.
privacy.introNote: We are a registered nonprofit organization. We comply with applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and other applicable international privacy regulations.
privacy.donationInfoDesc: Name, email address, billing address, and payment details. Cryptocurrency wallet addresses used for donations.
privacy.contactInfoDesc: Name and email address when you contact us, subscribe to newsletters, or request information.
privacy.volunteerAppsDesc: Name, contact details, skills, and availability.
privacy.usageDataDesc: Pages visited, time spent, referring URLs, and interaction patterns.
privacy.deviceInfoDesc: Browser type, operating system, screen resolution, and language settings.
privacy.ipAddressDesc: Used for security purposes (rate limiting, fraud prevention) and approximate geographic location. We do not use IP addresses for tracking or advertising.
privacy.s3_2: Send tax receipts and donation confirmations
privacy.s3_4: Respond to inquiries and support requests
privacy.s3_5: Send newsletters and updates (with your consent)
privacy.noSell: We never sell, rent, or trade your personal information.
privacy.noShare: We do not share donor information with other organizations for their fundraising or marketing purposes.
privacy.s4Text: Donations are accepted via cryptocurrency (USDT on the TRC-20 network). Blockchain transactions are publicly verifiable. We do not collect or store any personal financial information such as credit card numbers.
privacy.donationRecordsDesc: Retained for 7 years as required by applicable tax regulations for nonprofit organizations.
privacy.contactInquiriesDesc: Retained for 2 years from last communication.
privacy.securityLogsDesc: Retained for 90 days, then permanently deleted.
privacy.s6Intro: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to:
privacy.s6_1: Access the personal data we hold about you
privacy.s6_3: Request deletion of your data ("right to be forgotten")
privacy.s6_7: Lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority (for EU/EEA residents)
privacy.s6Contact: To exercise any of these rights, email info@iiran.org. We will respond within 30 days.
privacy.s7Text: We use only essential cookies required for website functionality (theme preference, session management). We do not use advertising cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party analytics that profile users. We do not participate in cross-site tracking.
privacy.s8VercelDesc: Website hosting. See Vercel Privacy Policy.
privacy.s9Text: Our website is not directed to children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If we become aware that a child has provided us with personal data, we will delete it immediately.
privacy.s10Text: Your information may be transferred to and maintained on servers located outside of your country of residence. We ensure appropriate safeguards are in place (Standard Contractual Clauses for EU/EEA data) to protect your information regardless of where it is processed.
privacy.s11Text: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of material changes by posting the new policy on this page and updating the "Last updated" date. We encourage you to review this page periodically.
privacy.s12Text: For privacy-related inquiries, contact our Data Protection Officer:

terms.s1Text: By accessing or using the IIRan website (iiran.org), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our website or services. IIRan is operated by IIRan, Inc., a registered nonprofit organization.
terms.s2_1Text: All donations to IIRan are voluntary and non-refundable. Donations are tax-deductible to the extent permitted by law under Section 170 of the Internal Revenue Code. You will receive an email confirmation and annual tax receipt for your records.
terms.s2_2Text: We allocate at least 90% of every donation directly to program services. Administrative and fundraising costs are kept below 10%. Detailed financial breakdowns are published in our annual reports.
terms.s2_3Text: If you choose to make a recurring monthly donation, you authorize us to charge your selected payment method on the same date each month. You may cancel a recurring donation at any time by emailing donate@iiran.org and we will process the cancellation promptly.
terms.s2_4Text: Donations are generally non-refundable. If you believe a donation was made in error (e.g., incorrect amount, unauthorized transaction), please contact us within 30 days at donate@iiran.org. We will review each request on a case-by-case basis.
terms.s3_1: Use automated tools to scrape or mine data from our website
terms.s3_2: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to our systems
terms.s3_3: Transmit malware, viruses, or malicious code
terms.s3_4: Interfere with or disrupt the website or connected infrastructure
terms.s3_6: Use the website for any unlawful purpose
terms.s4Text1: The IIRan name, logo, and original content on this website are protected by copyright and trademark law. News articles, stories, and media content from third-party sources are attributed to their respective creators and used under fair use or with permission.
terms.s4Text2: You may share our content for non-commercial purposes with appropriate attribution. For media use, please contact press@iiran.org.
terms.s5Text1: Our website and services are provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, either express or implied. We do not guarantee that the website will be uninterrupted, secure, or error-free.
terms.s5Text2: While we strive to provide accurate and up-to-date information, humanitarian situation data is sourced from multiple organizations and may contain delays or inaccuracies.
terms.s6Text: To the maximum extent permitted by law, IIRan and its directors, officers, employees, and volunteers shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the website or services.
terms.s7Text: Our website may contain links to third-party websites (news sources, partner organizations, payment processors). We are not responsible for the content, privacy practices, or availability of these external sites. Access them at your own risk.
terms.s8Text: These Terms are governed by applicable international law. Any disputes arising from these Terms shall be resolved through good-faith mediation or, if necessary, binding arbitration under internationally recognized procedures.
terms.s9Text: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Material changes will be posted on this page with an updated date. Continued use of the website after changes constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.
terms.s10Text: Questions about these Terms should be directed to:

transparency.heading: Where Your Money Goes
transparency.description: We publish complete financial data because transparency is the foundation of trust. Every dollar is tracked from donor to beneficiary. Our books are independently audited annually.
transparency.programSpendingDesc: Of $24.3M in total revenue, $22.2M (91.2%) was spent directly on humanitarian programs:
transparency.auditedReportsDesc: All financial statements are audited by independent certified accounting firms in accordance with international standards.
transparency.financialFilings: Financial filings and audit reports are available upon request at info@iiran.org.
transparency.quarterlyDesc: Every quarter, we publish a detailed report on how funds were spent and what outcomes were achieved.
transparency.questionsText: We welcome scrutiny. Transparency is not just a policy; it is our commitment to every donor.

brandLogos.description: Ready-to-use logos for social media platforms. Right-click and "Save as" to download, or use the direct links below. All files are SVG (infinitely scalable).

logoConc.description: 5 logo variants rendered at multiple sizes. Each works in monochrome and scales from favicon (24px) to hero (96px+).
logoConc.globeIranDesc: Globe with faint latitude/longitude lines and a simplified Iran silhouette. Instantly says 'this country, on the world stage.'
logoConc.interlockingIIDesc: Two vertical bars (the two I's in IIRan) linked by an arch and base. Typographic, geometric, distinctly branded.
logoConc.sunriseHandsDesc: Cupped hands cradling a rising sun. Symbolizes care, hope, and new beginnings.
logoConc.doveShieldDesc: Shield containing a stylized dove with olive branch. Protection + peace, universally understood.
logoConc.starHorizonDesc: Eight-pointed star (Persian geometric art) rising above a horizon. Cultural identity + dawn/recovery.
